violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded soup. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Fried rice was observed held between 126-130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated and turned up hot-hold unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, rice and soup were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC placed soup bucket in an ice bath and placed white rice in a shallow container. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
Inspector observed dead mouse in trap in basement area. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

Dishwasher was not in good repair. Per sanitizer service provider, dishwasher is not pulling sanitizer and instructed establishment to call dishwasher manufacture for service/parts. PIC called for service during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
The wall on the basement stairs is in poor repair (hole observed). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (peeler and mandolines) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC took unit out of service and will use the 3 bay sink to sanitize dishes. PIC called for service during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

White rice, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 56-59 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. PIC reheated rice to 167F during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Eggs and sprouts were observed held between 45-59 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed items in cooler for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Noodles were observed cooling on a shelf in the kitchen area. PIC moved container to walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
Inspector observed 2 dead mice and dead/trapped cockroaches in basement area. PIC to remove dead pests. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
Hot-hold unit located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Gaskets need repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer and prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The floors and walls in the basement area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The following areas are in poor repair: - ceilings in basement area (missing tiles), - wall along basement stairs (hole), - basement corner near stairs (corner molding coming off). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, juicer, meat grinder) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items taken out of service to clean. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Chicken was observed held between 64-73 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Minced garlic was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked chicken was observed cooling on the counter. PIC placed chicken in walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
Inspector observed rodent droppings beneath the stairs in the basement area. PIC informed to clean and maintain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

The hand-washing sink in the back kitchen area was blocked by table. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. PIC moved table. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
The bathrooms are lacking proper hand washing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

A scrub brush was observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed and moved scrub brush. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

The following food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils were observed with an accumulation of food and debris: -deli slicers; -juicer; -meat grinder; -mixer wand; -containers; -peeler; -mandoline; -ice machine. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items taken out of service until cleaned/sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

White rice was observed held between 79-110 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Fried chicken was observed cooling on the counter and chicken pieces cooling in deep pans in the walk-in cooler. PIC informed. Food items were broken down into smaller containers and placed in fridge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

The presence of rodents and flies is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings and fly traps. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler, walk-in freezer, kitchen area, and basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Inspector observed blender with lid that was not properly air dried. PIC notified. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the freezer chests and prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, ice, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Inspector observed some litter and debris around the refuse area. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Inspector observed grease build up and debris on top of the grease receptacle. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025

The walk-in freezer in the back kitchen area is in poor repair. Inspector observed a large frozen icicle indicating a leak. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
Inspector observed water stains in several ceiling tiles in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025
The ceilings in the basement area has an accumulation of cobwebs. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2025

Wood is used in the construction of seams in the dishwashing area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 11/22/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC to contact service. Sanitizer set-up being done at 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

The presence of rodents roaches are not controlled as evidenced by droppings and roach sightings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

Wood is used in the construction of the partition wall in the dishwashing area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the hood/ventilation unit have an accumulation of grease, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2023

Raw animal foods (beef ribs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (bagged noodles) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

Various food product in the walk in cooler was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in the basement, kitchen and shelving throughout the kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
Various Food product were observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
Inspector in use rice cooker being stored in the dishwasher area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
Inspector observed a sponge being stored above vegetables in the food prep sink. PIC informed and removed the sponge Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
Inspector observed the 3 bay prep sink used for food prep is lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023

The floors and shelving throughout the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris and rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
